mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which compresses corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid supply-side recovery that boosts near-term corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production surges fail to yield positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index reading exceeding 8.0 signals entrenched inflation that forces markets to anticipate restrictive monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 8.0 fail to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no predictive power for 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or positive payroll surprises coincide with negative equity returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains elevated, signals weakening consumer momentum that precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.03, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0% month-over-month during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization that boosts near-term earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal direction flips to negative in > 55% of triggered instances.
